items.author: Mike Lynch
items.body_excerpt: OG has also increased his regular season PER (15.8) to 24.6 in the postseason. His increase of 8.82 is the biggest postseason PER increase in history among any player with 500 postseason minutes played

items.title: Jeff Teague says OG Anunoby MUST WIN NBA Finals MVP over Jalen Brunson | Club 520 x Knicks
items.author: Club 520 Podcast
items.body_excerpt: Jeff Teague, DJ Wells, and B Hen react to OG Anunoby’s GAME-WINNER in Game 4 of NBA Finals, where the New York Knicks took a commanding 3-1 lead over the San Antonio Spurs. The guys talk about how OG has been the best player in the series, and Jeff says that he should win Finals…

items.title: Danny Green: The Spurs Needed a Leader in Game 4
items.author: Danny Green x Inside the Green Room
items.body_excerpt: De'Aaron Fox is supposed to be the veteran presence for the Spurs. But compared to most championship teams, he's still learning these moments himself. Danny Green breaks down why the San Antonio's inexperience may have finally caught up to them. Now it's win-or-go-home Saturday…

items.title: BRUNSON. OG. NBA FINALS HISTORY. 🔥
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
BRUNSON. OG. NBA FINALS HISTORY. 🔥

Jalen Brunson (36 PTS) and OG Anunoby (33 PTS, 19 in 2H, game-winner) combined for 69 points in Game 4 as New York erased a 29-point deficit to complete the LARGEST comeback in NBA Finals history!

items.title: OG Anunoby is now the playoff leader in Net Points per game at +4.8
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
OG Anunoby is now the playoff leader in Net Points per game at +4.8

Think of this like OG Anunoby is credited with adding +4.8 points to the Knicks scoring margin per game. He is positively impacting the score more than any other player in the playoffs

Screenshot: espnanalytics.com/nba-award-le...

images.alt:
Alt text:

Bar chart titled “MVP” with subtitle “Most Valuable Player – Highest Total Net Points, Best Overall.” Five players ranked by net points:

	1.	🏆 OG Anunoby, NYK +4.8
	2.	Karl-Anthony Towns, NYK +4.7
	3.	Victor Wembanyama, SAN +4.3
	4.	Jayson Tatum, BOS +3.3
	5.	Jalen Brunson, NYK +3.1

Anunoby and Towns rows are shaded blue (Knicks). Wembanyama’s row is white (Spurs). Tatum’s row is green (Celtics). Brunson’s row is blue (Knicks). Each row includes a player headshot and team logo.
